summary: The authors introduce AIR-MoE, a two-stage routing architecture using vector quantization to reduce the computational cost of granular Mixture-of-Experts models while improving performance.

blocks.content: # Adaptive Inverted-Index Routing for Granular Mixtures-of-Experts

source_span.quote: # Adaptive Inverted-Index Routing for Granular Mixtures-of-Experts

blocks.content: We introduce adaptive inverted-index routing for MoE (AIR-MoE), an inverted-index-inspired routing architecture based on vector quantization (VQ).

source_span.quote: We introduce adaptive inverted-index routing for MoE (AIR-MoE), an inverted-index-inspired routing architecture based on vector quantization (VQ).

blocks.content: Empirically, we demonstrate that AIR-MoE achieves improved performance compared to existing routing approaches in granular MoE settings.

source_span.quote: Empirically, we demonstrate that AIR-MoE achieves improved performance compared to existing routing approaches in granular MoE settings.

blocks.content: In a first stage, AIR-MoE performs coarse shortlisting by assigning tokens to VQ codewords to construct a candidate set of experts. In a second stage, fine scoring computes exact routing scores restricted to this shortlist.

source_span.quote: In a first stage, AIR-MoE performs coarse shortlisting by assigning tokens to VQ codewords to construct a candidate set of experts. In a second stage, fine scoring computes exact routing scores restricted to this shortlist.

blocks.content: AIR-MoE serves as a drop-in replacement for standard routers and requires no modifications to the model architecture or loss function.

source_span.quote: AIR-MoE serves as a drop-in replacement for standard routers and requires no modifications to the model architecture or loss function.

blocks.content: However, this regime substantially increases routing cost, which can dominate computation.

source_span.quote: However, this regime substantially increases routing cost, which can dominate computation.
